title: rinorea mollis
intro: Rinorea mollis, also known as the hairy-leaved rinorea or hairy-leaved viola, is a plant species in the family Violaceae, native to tropical Africa and Southeast Asia. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Rinorea mollis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and combat infections, making it a valuable remedy in traditional medicine. Rinorea mollis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, as well as being used in topical applications, such as creams and ointments.
